title: Label Detective
intro: A fund is sold as flexible and go-anywhere. Clues about its portfolio arrive one by one. Watch the allocation bar assemble — then diagnose what it really behaves like.

facts.label: Large-cap weight
facts.value: Roughly three-fifths of the book in large caps

facts.label: Mid & small motion
facts.value: Mid/small weights barely budged across a crash, recovery, and bull run

facts.label: Category marketing
facts.value: Advertised as free to roam the whole market-cap spectrum

facts.label: Peer contrast
facts.value: True multi-cap peers must hold at least 25% each in large, mid, and small

question: Best diagnosis for this case?
choices: A true multi-cap: floors force mid and small exposure
A flexi-cap that behaves like a large-cap fund in disguise
An index fund tracking Nifty Midcap 150
A debt fund with a temporary equity overlay
debrief: When freedom is unused, the label says flexi-cap while the risk/return path tracks large-cap-heavy exposure. That mismatch is the style-drift story — know what you own, not only what it is called.
